Circular collimation, 0.8 mm in diameter and
subtending an angle of 0.3 degrees, was used in the
measurement of data between 10 and 30 degrees (lab).
For angles greater than 30 degrees rectangular
collimators subtending an angle of 0.6 degrees were
used. Since alpha-particle groups from the
(6,7Li,alpha) reaction on the 12C backing were as
large as the 6,7Li elastic yield for detection angles
thicknesses were chosen to minimize the detection of

Absolute cross sections for all the
data were obtained in the following manner. First,
elastic proton scattering from the 90Zr and 91Zr
targets were measured for proton beam energies of 4.0
and 4.5 MeV. By assuming the cross section to be
Rutherford, the product of target angle times solid
angle, NdOmega, could be extracted and, therefore,
the absolute cross section. Then, without changing
the experimental geometry in any manner 34 MeV Li
elastic scattering was measured between 11 and 30
degrees lab. Since elastic scattering data were
measured simultaneously with the inelastic and
transfer data absolute cross sections could also be
microg/cm2 thick and mounted on a thin carbon backing.
